text: Now let's talk about Reliance, whose plan has always been the same. The industry in which you go, dominate that industry with the help of scale, speed and strategy. Reliance has said in its Q2FI26 con call that competition will be only in 10-30 cities with Blinket and others. Reliance Retail is present in almost 1000 plus cities. Reliance has also admitted in its con call that consumer behavior is changing and customers are more used to cucumbers now. So they are shifting from the next day delivery to a fast delivery 30-minute model. Reliance's existing retail infrastructure is primarily display and footfall based. And while quick commerce, business, speed, logistic optimization and micro fulfillment efficiency are more dependent on it. Blinket and Zepto have refined their models by entering into this industry and brought so much execution efficiency now that it is very tough to bring that efficiency for a new entrant. My personal viewpoint is that Reliance will definitely have a near term challenge with execution and distribution. Whereas in the long term, scale, capital and distribution can be strengthened and become a major player in that business. But even then market is fast growing, it's evolving and 93% unorganized players are still doing this. So, to organized players, there is a huge pie to be eaten there.
